channel.title: Comments on: Emerging Craft Beer Trends – Ep. 100
channel.description: Beer America TV is the first beer web site dedicated to the specialty craft brewing world with video on industry experts and insiders plus more!

item.title: By: TheBEERSgoneBAD
item.dc:creator: TheBEERSgoneBAD
item.description: Great show guys, I just watched a video on Almanac beer and they aged their beer in used wine barrels. Cool idea! Keep it up.
item.content:encoded: Great show guys, I just watched a video on Almanac beer and they aged their beer in used wine barrels. Cool idea! Keep it up.

item.title: By: Bobby
item.dc:creator: Bobby
item.description: Congrats on the 100th episode! Bring on the next 100!! The beer / wine blend sounds interesting. I'd like to see you do some Ohio brews like Great Lakes , Hoppin' Frog , and Thirsty Dog. I hear Georgia has Hoppin Frog now. The beers they are brewing are amazing.
item.content:encoded: Congrats on the 100th episode! Bring on the next 100!! The beer / wine blend sounds interesting. I’d like to see you do some Ohio brews like Great Lakes , Hoppin’ Frog , and Thirsty Dog. I hear Georgia has Hoppin Frog now. The beers they are brewing are amazing.

item.title: By: David
item.dc:creator: David
item.description:
Sorry I missed this episode ... just a couple other "bines" ..
Allagash has two beers which include actual grape juice. Victoria includes Chardonnay grapes (http://www.allagash.com/victoria.htm) and Victor has red chancellor grapes (http://www.allagash.com/victor.htm) These are both really interesting beers.
One of my favorite "Southern ingredient" beers is Lazy Magnolia's Southern Pecan Nut Brown Ale (http://www.lazymagnolia.com/ourbeer.php#bpbeer1).
I'm sure there are many others and hopefully some of our viewers will help us find more.
David
item.content:encoded:
Sorry I missed this episode … just a couple other “bines” ..

Allagash has two beers which include actual grape juice. Victoria includes Chardonnay grapes (http://www.allagash.com/victoria.htm) and Victor has red chancellor grapes (http://www.allagash.com/victor.htm) These are both really interesting beers.

One of my favorite “Southern ingredient” beers is Lazy Magnolia’s Southern Pecan Nut Brown Ale (http://www.lazymagnolia.com/ourbeer.php#bpbeer1).

I’m sure there are many others and hopefully some of our viewers will help us find more.

David
